name: Gitpod API
what_it_does: Provides programmatic control over Gitpod cloud development environments, enabling agents to create, start, stop, and manage ephemeral workspaces tied to git repositories.
use_cases: Spin up a pre-configured dev environment for a pull request review without local setup
Automate workspace creation for onboarding pipelines that provision environments per-developer
Monitor and shut down idle workspaces to control cloud spend
List and audit all running workspaces across an organization for compliance reporting
Trigger workspace builds to validate that a repository's devcontainer configuration is functional
not_for: Running arbitrary long-lived server processes — workspaces time out when idle
Persistent database hosting — ephemeral nature means data is lost unless explicitly persisted
Replacing CI/CD runners for heavy build pipelines — cost and latency are higher than dedicated CI
best_when: An agent needs to provision a fully pre-configured, browser-accessible coding environment from a git URL without managing local infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need long-running stateful compute or sub-second workspace start times, as cold starts can take 30-90 seconds.

auth.notes: Personal Access Tokens (PATs) are the primary method for agent use. OAuth2 is available for user-delegated flows. PATs are scoped to workspace read/write and org-level access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 50 hours/month of workspace usage on the free plan
pricing.paid_tiers: Individual paid plans start around $9/month for more hours; Team plans scale by seat
pricing.notes: Free tier is sufficient for light agent experimentation. Heavy automation scenarios require a paid plan.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; generous for typical API usage, workspace creation is the bottleneck (30-90s per workspace)

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: gRPC errors include status codes but REST error bodies are sometimes generic; workspace state transitions can fail silently if the underlying VM is unhealthy.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Workspace creation by context URL is idempotent in practice (reuses running workspace), but the API does not guarantee this; stopping an already-stopped workspace returns an error rather than a no-op.
known_agent_gotchas: Workspace start is asynchronous — polling workspace status is required; there is no webhook to signal readiness
Free-tier workspaces are rate-limited on concurrent count (typically 4), causing 429s during burst creation
Context URLs must be exact git refs; malformed URLs produce unhelpful 400 errors without field-level detail
The REST API and gRPC API have feature parity gaps — some workspace controls only exist in gRPC
Personal Access Tokens expire; agents must handle 401s and surface token rotation to operators

security_score_components.security_notes: All traffic over TLS. PATs can be scoped but granularity is coarse (workspace vs org). No IP allowlisting on free tier.
